opps.title: NIH NICHD OIT Application and Website Support Services
opps.description:
The NICHD Office of Information Technology  requires continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance, Section 508 Compliance, database and data mart support, application infrastructure support, and other technical services including but not limited to the support of new and emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine learning (ML)/Robotic Process Automation (RPA), NICHD and/or NIH Wide. The focus of these IT support activities is creation, development, maintenance, operation, and support of a web application infrastructure that provides an efficient, effective, reliable, and secure computing environment in support of NICHD's and NIH's mission and supports staff in the efficient and effective utilization of these capabilities. 

Details on the evaluation criteria are contained in the attached RFQ.

In order to be considered for award, a vendor will be required to cover the SIN(s) listed below awarded on their Schedule contract. Any quote received from a contractor that does not cover the required SIN(s) will not be further evaluated and subsequently ineligible for award. Required SIN(s): 	54151S (Information Technology Professional Services); 541611 (Management and Financial Consulting, Acquisition and Grants Management Support, and Business Program and Project Management Services)
opps.summary:
Background

The goal of this contract is to ensure Section 508 Compliance, provide database and data mart support, and offer application infrastructure support, including the integration of new technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML).

opps.title: Application Development and Support Services - FMCS
opps.description:
The General Services Administration, Office of Centralized Acquisition Services, on behalf of the Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service, is issuing this Request for Quote to establish a single-award Blanket Purchase Agreement under GSA Multiple Award Schedule SIN 54151S Information Technology Professional Services.

FMCS requires Application Development and Support Services to maintain, support, modernize, secure, integrate, and enhance its enterprise application environment. The FMCS environment includes Microsoft 365, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Platform, Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power BI, Dataverse, LMS365, Azure Government / GCC Medium, custom .NET/C# applications, SQL Server, PostgreSQL where applicable, Collabspace Enterprise Records Management, CCMS, Arbitration systems, the FMCS public web environment, AWS-hosted workloads, and legacy systems.

The scope includes project management, operations and maintenance, minor enhancements, major enhancements, application development, modernization and migration support, cloud migration support, cybersecurity compliance support, ATO/POA&M support, Section 508/accessibility support, records management support, documentation, reporting, quality control, transition support, and related technical services.

The BPA will include the following service CLINs:

CLIN 0001 Project Management
CLIN 0002 Operations and Maintenance and Minor Enhancements
CLIN 0003 Major Enhancements, Modernization, and Migration
The Government anticipates issuing Initial BPA Call 0001 concurrently with BPA award for Base Period ADSS Services. The anticipated BPA period of performance consists of one 12-month base period and three 12-month option periods. The estimated BPA ceiling is $4,500,000. The BPA does not obligate funds; funds will be obligated only through BPA Calls issued by the Contracting Officer.

This RFQ is a 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside under GSA MAS SIN 54151S. Only quotes submitted by GSA Sch

opps.title: Power Platform Development Support for Office of Emergency Management
opps.description:
The Department of the Interior (DOI), Interior Business Center (IBC), Acquisition Services Directorate (AQD), is conducting market research on behalf of the Office of Emergency Management (OEM). 

OEM relies on Microsoft Power Platform, SharePoint, PowerApps, Power Automate, and Dataverse for mission-critical emergency management operations including incident reporting, resource tracking, workflow automation, and continuity of operations support. AQD is seeking information from qualified vendors capable of supporting the development, modernization, and sustainment of these solutions in a federal environment.

Vendors with demonstrated experience in Power Platform development, Application Lifecycle Management (ALM), Microsoft Graph API integration, Section 508 compliance, and federal IT security standards are encouraged to respond.

Please review ALL attached documents before responding, including the Request for Information (RFI) and the DOI Information Technology Baseline Compliance Contract Guidelines. 

All submission requirements, capability areas of interest, and response instructions are detailed in the attached RFI.

Responses are due by July 9, 2026 11:00 AM Eastern.

All responses must be submitted in PDF format via email to Candy_Mott-Harris@ibc.doi.gov with the subject line: RFI Response OEM Power Platform Development Support.

opps.title: Video Production Professional Staffing
opps.description:
The Department of State's Bureau of Global Public Affairs (GPA), Office of Video (GPA/DC/VID), is soliciting proposals for Digital Video Staffing Services (DVDS) in support of its mission to communicate U.S. foreign policy priorities to domestic and global audiences. The contractor shall provide a professional staff of approximately 2550 personnel to conceive, produce, and deliver high-quality video, broadcast, and live interactive web content across digital and traditional media platforms worldwide. Services span the full production lifecycle from pre-production planning and scripting to post-production editing and global live broadcast origination and must be executed in an agile, responsive manner consistent with the fast-paced demands of a news-style environment.

The scope of work encompasses three primary task areas: (1) Professional Digital Media Production, including social media video, motion graphics, and documentary-style content; (2) Digital Broadcast, Interactive, and Live Streaming, including studio operations, live coverage of Department press briefings, and Secretary-level engagements; and (3) Video Broadcast Infrastructure Maintenance and Technology Services, including procurement, maintenance, and support of broadcast and IT equipment and software subscriptions. Contractor personnel must be willing to travel domestically and internationally on short notice, work nights and weekends as required, and maintain constant readiness to support time-sensitive tasking. Performance will be conducted primarily at the Department's headquarters at 2201 C Street, NW, Washington, D.C.

Please see all attachments included with this RFP for full details, technical requirements, performance standards, and contractual specifications governing this acquisition. Offerors are responsible for reviewing all attached documents prior to submitting a proposal.

All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officers at HofhineAK@sta

Design staff will create visual assets that highlight and elevate USFAâ€™s brand, reinforcing its position as a trusted leader in fire and emergency medical services. Visual arts support services are expected to recommend best design practices.

See attached RFQ 140E0126Q0028.

opps.title: Accelerated Constituent Service Solutions
opps.description:
The Government is seeking industry feedback on a DRAFT Master Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) for Accelerated Constituent Service Solutions. The purpose of this Request for Information (RFI) is to obtain comments, recommendations, and suggested revisions from industry to help refine the DRAFT BPA structure, scope, requirements, ordering procedures, and related terms prior to any potential solicitation.

The contemplated BPA is intended to provide The General Services Administration (GSA) with a streamlined ordering mechanism for the licensing, configuration, technical delivery, integration, and support of secure digital platform solutions. These solutions would enable agencies to rapidly develop and deploy production-ready, constituent-facing applications within their existing authorized cloud environments and security boundaries. Potential use cases may include, but are not limited to, digital forms, identity and access capabilities, case management, payment functionality, workflow automation, reporting, and other mission-specific digital services.

Through this RFI, the Government is particularly interested in industry perspectives on how best to structure the BPA to support rapid delivery, scalability, security, interoperability, and flexibility across diverse agency missions. Respondents are encouraged to identify areas of the DRAFT BPA that may benefit from clarification, recommend commercial best practices, highlight potential barriers to competition or performance, and propose approaches that would improve the Government's ability to acquire and deploy secure, user-centered digital services efficiently.

The Government's objective is to establish a BPA that supports the delivery of fully operational, production-ready constituent-facing applications, allowing agencies to stand up mission-specific services quickly while maintaining compliance with applicable security, privacy, accessibility, and cloud authorization requirements. Industry feedback received in r

opps.title: Common API Services (CAS)
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization provides enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities that enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems. These capabilities reduce duplication, lower risk and cost, and accelerate delivery of modernization initiatives.

The D&PE API Services organization requires contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain mission-critical middleware applications and shared services that support tax processing, taxpayer services, and other IRS mission-essential functions.

The objective of the Performance Work Statement (PWS) is to:

-	Modernize legacy middleware platforms, including webMethods Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), Bulk Request Management Gateway (BRMG), and Business Rules Engine (BRE), into microservices-based architectures using Spring Boot and OpenShift standards 
-	Maintain uninterrupted operations during transition 
-	Modernize common business services (APIs) and sustain existing services throughout the modernization efforts
-	Upgrade applications to current Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) versions and/or OpenShift (Enterprise Container Platform (ECP)

The IRS requires full lifecycle support (analysis, development, testing, and deployment) for applications hosted in IRS on-premise environments. This support includes delivery of software releases aligned to IRS Forms processing, modernization initiatives, and legislative requirements.

The D&PE API Services organization seeks contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain critical middleware applications and shared services

opps.title: USACE UMSB Army Vantage Integration Field Service Representative (FRS)
opps.description: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, USACE Mission Support Battalion (UMSB) is executing a directed modernization initiative to transition operational workflows from manual, locally managed processes to structured, enterprise-integrated systems aligned with Army Vantage and the Microsoft ecosystem. This effort supports federal and Army digital transformation mandates requiring measurable progress in data consolidation, automated reporting, and enterprise visibility. One (1) senior-level Field Service Representative (FSR) will need to be embedded at Humphreys Engineer Center, Alexandria, Virginia, with periodic presence at a secondary Government location in Washington, DC beginning in Month 4. The FSR shall perform duties consistent with the approved Performance Work Statement (PWS) across five task areas: backend integration support, workflow documentation and transition support, user training and adoption support, integration status reporting, and stakeholder engagement and coordination. The FSR shall provide on-site technical and change management support, working directly with workflow owners, UMSB leadership, and the UMSB Operations (OPS) office. The overarching program objective for the base period is to achieve the HR Integration End State as defined in Section 3 of the Performance Work Statement (PWS), establishing a replicable integration model for future directorate-level transitions. Leadership's primary operational objective is to utilize Army Vantage's built-in templated dashboard functions, populated from authoritative HR data sources, as the standard method for HR data visibility and decision support, replacing manual data pulls and locally managed reporting.

opps.title: Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering Organization requires contractor support to modernize, automate, operate, and sustain the Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).

Under this effort, the Contractor shall provide full lifecycle technical services, including design, development, configuration, modernization, operations, and maintenance of the Enterprise API Gateway environment. 

An Application Programming Interface (API) is a defined set of rules and protocols that enables different software systems to communicate and exchange data, and an API gateway is a centralized service that manages, secures, routes, and monitors API traffic between providers and consumers. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of operations during the transition and maintain support for existing APIs while implementing modernization improvements.

The end products of this contract will include a modernized Enterprise API Gateway operating model which consists of:

-	Automated API onboarding and Continuous Integration /Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines
-	Enterprise API governance artifacts and standards
-	Security controls aligned with Zero Trust principles
-	Developer enablement documentation and tools
-	Integration with various Artificial Intelligence (AI) providers, consumers, and/or gateways
-	Sustained production operations and performance monitoring for the duration of the contract
The results of this contract will be used to improve system interoperability, standardize API lifecycle management, enhance cybersecurity, and support IRS modernization and mission delivery requirements.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
After the completion of the State Laws digest on illegal passing, a more in-depth review or state of knowledge report is needed to determine if a model law for illegal passing would help with the consistency of enforcement of these driving violations. More clarity if needed on how currently laws are enforced to help better inform states to improve enforcement of these laws. The state laws digest informed that laws vary in content, organization, consistency, and clarity. Also, that what may constitute illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neigh-boring one. States also vary in their methods to review, document and report violations including permissibility of stop-arm cameras. This report is needed to give clarity on how states can best improve enforcement of this law.

To address this need, this project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This study will provide if a model law would be necessary for continuity among enforcement within the U.S States and provide best example of a model law.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is seeking to enhance the enforcement of laws against the illegal passing of school buses, a significant safety concern for students. 

The project aims to address inconsistencies in enforcement and prosecution across various jurisdictions, as current laws vary widely in their content and clarity. A previous digest indicated that illegal passing violations are often dismissed in court, highlighting the need for improved education and advocacy among judicial officials. This solicitation seeks to conduct a comprehensive revie

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source - Quorum Analytics
opps.agency: DHS Office of the Chief Procurement Officer
opps.description:
This is a notice of intent to solicit and award a sole source contract in accordance with the acquisition of commercial products and commercial services under the simplified acquisition threshold in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.102, Restricting Competition.  

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) hereby issues this notice of intent to award a sole source contract to Quorum Analytics, LLC, 1 Thomas Circle, NW, 6th Floor, Washington, DC 20005.

 Background

To fulfill its mission, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) requires access to specialized scientific journals and databases to stay current with emerging research and developments. Quorum is a leading platform for federal bill tracking, social media tracking, and a legislative engagement platform. By acquiring Quorum, DHS S&T staff will gain real-time access to government data, monitor legislative activity, and conduct comprehensive policy analysis. This capability will strengthen operational effectiveness, support informed decision-making, and streamline research efforts, ultimately advancing mission-critical objectives. S&T will procure five (5)  Quorum subscriptions for (5) users.

Quorum is the sole provider of an integrated platform that offers real-time federal bill tracking, social media monitoring, and legislative engagement capabilities. No other vendor delivers this unique combination of services with the same breadth, depth, and real-time functionality.

Quorum is uniquely positioned to meet DHS S&T’s needs for the following reasons:

Exclusive Integrated Platform: Quorum is the only platform that combines federal bill tracking, social media tracking, and legislative engagement in a single, unified system. This integration is not available from any other vendor.

Real-Time Access: Quorum provides

opps.description:
U.S. Coast Guard (USCG) Headquarters Formal Contracts Division (OL-CG-HCA-COCO-1) invites you to submit a quote via Indefinite-Delivery Contracts (cf. Federal Acquisition Regulation [FAR] Part 16.5), to provide Integrated Program Management and Information Technology Services for the Coast Guard Accessions Service Center (CG ASC). This solicitation document and incorporated provisions and clauses are those in effect through Federal Acquisition Circular 2022-08 Effective date: 23 September 2022.

opps.title: Network Engineers and Specialists Support (NE&S)
opps.description:
Non-personal services to provide network sustainment and deployment support services to the Military Health System (MHS) Medical Treatment Facilities (MTFs) both inside and outside the continental United States (CONUS and OCONUS). This work includes functions such as network performance
measurement and monitoring, assistance with network design and development, network measurement, customer service in the form of trouble tracking and troubleshooting at the Tier 0 level, onsite support for both contractor and Government support teams for escalated trouble tickets, as well as maintenance and sparing support. Such services will be provided in accordance with stated MHS and specific Medical Component priorities. Services shall also comply with existing and evolving technical architecture guidance from international, commercial, DoD, and Health Affairs (HA) sources.  These services will be provided in a centralized and decentralized mode. The on-site network specialists will be the primary 24/7 on-call decentralized points of contact within the MTFs. Additional support and guidance will be provided by regional network engineers and DHA centralized infrastructure team.

See Section M of the attached solicitation for evaluation criteria.

opps.title: GSA - Promoting the Administration's Domestic Sourcing/Buy American Goals - MRAS
opps.description:
GSA Wants Your Feedback to Enhance Domestic Preference in GSA Acquisition Programs - Market Research

In accordance with Executive Order 14392 "Ensuring Truthful Advertising of Products Claiming to be Made in America" and in order to advance the Administration's policy to revitalize American manufacturing and promote American workers, the General Services Administration (GSA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI). The RFI seeks input from industry stakeholders on how to regenerate an important part of the American economy by making it easier to buy American products through GSA Advantage. Only responses submitted through the RFI link below will be accepted. Responses submitted through any other means will NOT be evaluated or recorded.

RFI Link: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_cvEH8Pm0kAwRYfY

(NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the link into a new browser.)

Additional Information:
Your company may receive multiple RFI notifications for this requirement since it is being posted on GSA eBuy, SAM.gov. and marketed widely on GSA platforms. Your company only needs to complete the RFI survey one time.

Any questions regarding this RFI can be sent to: rfi@research.gsa.gov. Please include the RFI number in your email title.

opps.title: Notification of Intent to Award a Sole Source Purchase Order to LexisNexis
opps.description:
This notice is being published in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Subpart 5.101 requiring dissemination of information for proposed contract actions. This is a notice of intent to award a sole source purchase order. THIS NOTICE IS NOT A REQUEST FOR COMPETITIVE PROPOSALS.

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) intends to negotiate and award a sole-source purchase order to LexisNexis for StateNet. The Government intends to procure this requirement in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Part 12, specifically RFO FAR 12.102(a), for acquisitions valued at or below the simplified acquisition threshold (SAT).

The period of performance for this requirement is a Base year and four (4) 1-year Option years.

The CFTC requires a tool to track state legislation concerning prediction markets with real-time alerts and forecasting capabilities. StateNet provides comprehensive coverage of legislation, regulations, and policy activity across all 50 states, Congress, and key U.S. territories. StateNet provides access to bills, rules, statutes, executive orders, legislative hearings, regulatory notices, and rulemaking activity. The CFTC is unaware of any alternative solutions that can meet the agency’s requirements.

This notice is not a competitive request for proposals.  A competitive solicitation will not be issued. Interested parties who believe they can provide these services may submit a capabilities statement to the Contracting Officer no later than July 8, 2026, at 10:00 AM Eastern Time. Responses must be submitted via email. Information received will be considered solely for the purpose of Market Research. A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed contract based upon responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

The Government will not be responsible for any costs of preparation in response to this announcement.
win_reasons: Full & Open — competing against all firm sizes; lower win probability without incumbent advantage
No direct capability lane match found — scope may not align with BLN24 proven delivery
⚠️ Scope gap: Cybersecurity — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes

capability_matrix.fit_reason: No task areas identified in scope text — review solicitation directly

qualifying.answer: Warning: "gis" — area where BLN24 has limited PP (debrief data)

opps.title: 2030 Decennial Administrative Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS)
opps.description:
The Census Integrated Personnel and Payroll System (CIPPS) is an integrated suite of government and vendor systems for recruiting, assessing, fingerprinting, background clearance, hiring, onboarding, benefits, and training temporary employees* in support of the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census. 

The purpose of this requirement is to obtain technical contractor services for the 2030 Decennial Administrative, Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS). The 2030 DARHTS is the primary Census system for automating, standardizing, and streamlining of job applications and assessments during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. This requirement will also obtain a robust, integrated training solution and Help Desk (optional) operations during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. The provided training solution shall utilize a FedRAMP (Moderate or Higher) compliant Software as a Service (SaaS) solution.

Note that the Solicitation along with Attachments 1 through 3 and 5 through 14 are attached. Attachment 4 is not included with this solicitation and will be provided after award.

Note that this is a Hybrid Type Contract with Firm Fixed Price (FFP) components as well as Time and Material (T&M) components.

opps.title: Maintaining Momentum in More Than a Job States
opps.description:
This Request for Quotation (RFQ) is prepared in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 8.4 and General Service Administration Regulation (GSAR) 538.71, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice.  Proposed pricing must be offered against the contractor's current schedule contract.

FNA is seeking a contractor to provide expert technical assistance to States to expand and improve their SNAP E&T programs. The period of performance is not to exceed a 12-month base period from the date of contract award. The Contractor shall accomplish the following over the period of performance:
-	Deliver program and policy related technical assistance to up to fifteen (15) States that have previously engaged or are planning to engage with FNA to develop a More Than a Job Campaign. The purpose of the technical assistance is to strengthen State SNAP E&T programs, increase their effectiveness, and address policy and program challenges that hinder successful implementation of the More Than a Job campaign. 
-	Develop and disseminate the SNAP E&T Review, a periodic digest of SNAP E&T relevant events, announcements and other news relevant to SNAP E&T.

opps.title: Committee on Uniform Security Identification Procedures (CUSIP) Enterprise License - Notice of Intent to Sole Source
opps.agency: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
opps.description:
The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) hereby announces its intent to solicit and enter into a sole source contract with FactSet Research System, Inc 45 Glover Ave, Suite 700, FL 7, Norwalk, CT 06850, in accordance with FAR RFO 12.102 (a) only one source is reasonably available valued at or below the simplified acquisition threshold.  This notice complies with FAR RFO 5.101 pre-solicitation notice.

The OCC requires an enterprise license subscription to access Committee on Uniform Security Identification Procedures (CUSIP).  A CUSIP is a nine-digit number or nine-character alphanumeric code that identifies a North American financial instrument, including stocks and bonds, facilitating trading, clearance and settlement. CUSIP is managed by FactSet Research Systems, Inc on behalf of the American Bankers Association. The license enables the OCC to use CUSIPs embedded within OC data products and third-party data products for OCC related research and analytical efforts.  The base period of performance is 09.30.26 – 09.29.27, with four 12-month option periods.

This is not a request for competitive quotes; however, the Government will consider responses from all responsible sources received no later than 12:00 PM Eastern Time on July 10, 2026. Responses must be supported with clear and convincing evidence to clearly be able to provide the services outlined in this notice. Any responses received will only be considered for the purpose of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement in lieu of the sole source described in this notice, and such determination shall be made at the sole discretion of the OCC. 

Responses must be submitted in writing to Danielle Pena via email at Danielle.pena@occ.treas.gov, no later than the timeframe noted above.  Telephone responses and requests will not be received.

⚠️ Scope gap: Cybersecurity — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes

When holding the meetings in-person is not feasible due to travel limitations and/or grantee safety concerns, the meetings will be held virtually as necessary.

opps.title: Sources Sought for News Monitoring Services
opps.description:
1. Purpose 

This Request for Information (RFI) is issued for market research purposes only in accordance with FAR 10.001. It does not constitute a solicitation, a request for proposal, or a commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or award a contract. No reimbursement will be made for any costs associated with providing information in response to this RFI.

The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is conducting market research to identify vendors capable of providing comprehensive news monitoring services.

The information gathered through this RFI will be used to determine the appropriate acquisition strategy, set-aside eligibility, and procurement approach, including but not limited to full and open competition, small business set-aside, or sole source justification.

2. Requested Information

Interested vendors are requested to provide the following information. Responses should be concise and limited to 10 pages or fewer.

2.1 Company Information Cover sheet that includes: 


	Legal company name, address, DUNS/UEI number, and CAGE code;
	SAM.gov registration status and expiration date
	Points of contact including phone number and e-mail address;
	Small business status and applicable socioeconomic categories (e.g., 8(a), WOSB, HUBZone, SDVOSB, VOSB, SB)
	Business size standard under the applicable NAICS code
	Applicable Government-wide Acquisition Contracts (GWACs), Multiple Award Contracts (MACs), or GSA Schedule contracts (if any)


2.1 Capability Statement


	Description of your firm's experience providing news monitoring services to Federal Government clients in accordance to the attached Draft Statement of Work


​​​​​​​2.3 Pricing Information (Rough Order of Magnitude Only)


	For market research purposes only, provide a rough order of magnitude (ROM) pricing estimate for a base year


​​​​​​​2.4 Draft Statement of Work (SOW) Comments

A draft Statement of Work (SOW) for this requirement is attached to this RFI as Attachment A. The

opps.title: STRATEGIC INDUSTRIAL BASE SERVICES (SIBS)
opps.description:
This is a Request for Information (RFI) for market research purposes only. This is not a solicitation for proposals, proposal abstracts, or quotations. The purpose of this RFI is to identify sources with the capability to provide the highly specialized strategic industrial base services detailed in the attached draft Performance Work Statement (PWS).

The Office of the Assistant Secretary of the Air Force for Acquisition, Technology and Logistics (SAF/AQ) is seeking information on contractors who can provide support services including, but not limited to, defense industrial base intelligence, continuous financial forensics, Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE) analysis, and enterprise negotiation strategy. The objective is to integrate the intersection of Government Contracting (GovCon) law and corporate financial drivers to mitigate program risk and optimize acquisition planning for the Department of the Air Force (DAF).

The Government will not award a contract on the basis of this RFI, nor will it reimburse any costs associated with preparing a response. Responses to this RFI are voluntary and will be used to determine the appropriate acquisition strategy for this requirement.

Please carefully review the attached RFI and PWS before providing a response.

opps.title: Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC)
opps.description: Air Forice Office of Special Investigation (AFOSI) has a requirement for  The Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC) integrates and audits criminal history records from law enforcement and judge advocate professionals to deliver accurate and timely criminal history records to external stakeholders ensuring public safety and the rights of the individual.  The organization is aligned under the commander of the DAF Office of Special Investigations (OSI) and serves as the primary focal point for the exchange of criminal justice information between DAF entities and US and foreign government criminal justice jurisdictions.  This contract requirement increases capacity of a training team, an audit team, an operations team, and a mission support team.  The Contractor shall interface and perform technical and programmatic liaison support across the DAF-CJIC and with DAF Headquarters elements conducting or interfacing with indexing or criminal justice information management requirements, operations, and planning.  The Contractor shall provide support to perform information and records management utilizing multiple government databases to request, receive, inventory, and prepare information for entry into criminal justice databases internal and external to the DAF.  Additionally, the Contractor shall support related administrative functions in support of personnel assigned to the DAF-CJIC, various DAF entities that accomplish indexing operations, and/or indexing task forces IAW the PWS.

opps.description:
This is a non-personal services task order to provide A&AS Engineering Support Services. The Government shall not exercise any supervision or control over the contract services providers performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government. 18 CEG requires assistance to provide highly reliable, highly credible professionals, with specialized technical expertise to support the 718 CES Engineering, Installation Management, and Housing Maintenance Flights as well as 18 CES Operations Flight. Contractor will provide planning, programming, project management, environmental support, electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, civil engineering, structural engineering, architectural and inspection support on site to 718 CES/CEN including CEN's GOJ section (718 CES/CENPJ), 718 CES/CEI, 718 CES/CEL, and 18 CES/CEO Flights. opps.title: Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Case Management System (CMS)
opps.description: The DHA Office of Equal Employment and Resolution Management (OEERM) office requires this automated tracking system to ensure accurate management and oversight of EEO Complaints for the Agency or the production of the annual 462 report in accordance with regulatory compliance. The goal of this requirement is to continue to maintain this commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) product (Case Management Development Platform powered by Entellitrak) in order to provide a system capable of ensuring the agency is within regulatory compliance. Similar products will be implemented for other capabilities such as Anti-Harassment (AH). This COTS solution will continue to be hosted on Military Cloud (milCloud) development, test and production environments. Additional responsibility for this effort will be to provide operations, sustainment and engineering support as well as system updates and maintenance of all non-production environments.

opps.title: Integrated Document Management System (IDMS) Upgrade Services
opps.description:
Hanford Mission Integration Solutions, LLC. (HMIS) requests proposals for Integrated Document Management System (IDMS) Upgrade Services.

The requested work is in support of Hanford Mission Essential Services Contract (HMESC) Prime Contract 89303320DEM000031 with the U.S. Department of Energy, Richland Office.

Information regarding the submission of a proposal is contained in the attached Solicitation.  Proposals are to be prepared in accordance with the instructions and conditions set forth herein.  
	
- Notification of Intent to Propose due date: July 10, 2026 or sooner
- Questions and Comments due date: July 16, 2026 at 1:00 PM (PT) or sooner
- New Proposals due date: July 29, 2026 at 2:00 PM (PT)

Pre-Proposal Conference: Scheduled for July 21, 2026, from 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM (PT). This will be conducted via Microsoft Teams. Offerors interested in participating shall notify the Procurement Specialist via email. Conference details will be provided for interested offerors a few days prior to the scheduled event.

Presentation Timeline: After proposal review and determination of selected Offeror (down select list); Procurement will reach out to schedule the project presentations within two (2) weeks of proposal due date.  Please ensure your availability during this period to facilitate the scheduling of the presentation.

All questions are to be directed to the Procurement Specialist.  All proposals are subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein.  Any exceptions, deviations, or omissions may be grounds for rejection of proposals submitted.

HMIS looks forward to your response.

Thank you,

Joann Carter
HMIS Procurement Specialist
Email: joann_carter@rl.gov

opps.title: NIJ-NamUs (National Missing and Unidentified Persons System)
opps.description:
NOTE 1: RFQ submission due date is MONDAY, JUNE 29, 2026 by 12:00PM ET.
Submit all quotations by MONDAY, JUNE 29, 2026 at 12:00PM ET.
GSA eBuy system stated close date of Wednesday, July 29, 2026 by 12:00PM ET is INCORRECT.
NOTE 2: ---RFQ1814958 (100% small business set-aside) HAS BEEN CANCELED-- 
RFQ1815768 (unrestricted) REPLACES RFQ 1814958--- Submit quotations to RFQ1815768 only.

National Institute of Justice National Missing and Unidentified Persons System (NIJ-NamUs) REQUEST FOR QUOTATION (RFQ). The goals and objectives of this effort are for the NamUs program to:
(1) operate without interruption,
(2) provide authorization, access, technical support, and training to users of the NamUs databases, and
(3) provide free forensic services to resolve NamUs cases.

The goal of the NamUs program is to work with NIJ to assist federal, state, local and tribal law enforcement, medical examiners and coroners, allied professionals, and the general public with resolving missing, unidentified, and unclaimed persons cases. The scope of this effort is to obtain professional services to provide core elements in support of the NamUs program.

Specific tasks for this effort are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS and will be managed by the successful vendor. The successful vendor will work closely with NIJ program scientists and interface with other OJP Bureaus and Offices, as well as the contractors who support them. Services covered within the scope of this effort as well as the specific tasks for this anticipated contract are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS include but are not limited to:
TASK AREA 1: Provide Qualified Leadership and Staff.
TASK AREA 2: Provide Case Management and Investigative Support
TASK AREA 3: Provide Forensic/Biometric Services to assist with Identifying Remains
TASK AREA 4: Communications
TASK AREA 5: Transfer and Transition of NamUs Program
---PLEASE SEE ALL ATTACHMENTS---

opps.title: IFMS Services & Support
opps.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
opps.description:
The purpose of this contract is to provide maintenance and support for Information Technology System Services to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) Office of Safety Security and Asset Management (OSSAM) for the Integrated Facilities Management System (IFMS). This includes sustainability of the overall system, its customized modules and custom integration applications and programs as well as revising, testing, documenting, operating and maintaining the Facilities Management Information (FMI) parts of IFMS.

The assigned components of the Government's Integrated Facilities Management System will be maintained and enhanced as necessary to support OSSAM operations, operability and coordination among various TRIRIGA and non-TRIRIGA software applications.
CDC and end users will be provided high quality training and support.

Please see attached RFQ for additional information. Questions should be received at jpowell5@cdc.gov NLT June 10, 2026 at 3:00 p.m.EST. Quotes are due to be received via GSA E-Buy no later than July 30, 2026 at 3:00 p.m. EST

opps.title: Access Required to Online Real Estate Database Services
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial items/services prepared in accordance with the format in Federal Acquisition Regulations (FAR) subpart 12.6, “Streamlined Procedures for Evaluation and Solicitation for Commercial Items or Services”, and FAR Part 13 Simplified Acquisition Procedures, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; quotes are being requested. This solicitation number is 2043FY-26-Q-00038 and is issued as a Request for Quote (RFQ). 

The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) is seeking commercial real estate database subscription services to support the Large Business and International (LB&I) Division's Engineering Program.

The LB&I Division’s Engineering Program provides technical support on engineering and valuation issues involving large and complex business tax returns. Specialists perform work involving commercial real estate appraisal, machinery and equipment valuation, cost segregation studies, and research credit claims.

To support these activities, the Government requires access to a commercial real estate information platform that provides nationwide current and historical market data. The platform must provide comprehensive commercial real estate information, including ownership records, sales transactions, lease information, market trends, and related market analytics necessary to evaluate commercial properties and support tax administration activities. The Government intends to award a contract to the responsible offeror whose quotation conforms to the solicitation requirements and offers the lowest evaluated price. This requirement is unrestricted for full and open competition.

The Statement of Work and all applicable solicitation documents are included as attachments to this notice. Sources who believe they can provide the products/services included in the Statement of Work (SOW) shall submit a price quote via email

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source - Bloomberg Government Subscription Service
opps.agency: DHS Office of the Chief Procurement Officer
opps.description:
This is a notice of intent to solicit and award a sole source contract in accordance with the acquisition of commercial products and commercial services under the simplified acquisition threshold in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.102, Restricting Competition.  

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) hereby issues this notice of intent to award a sole source contract to Bloomberg Industry Group, Inc, 1801 South Bell Street Arlington, VA 22202.

 Background

In support of the S&T mission, many divisions need access to certain scientific journals and databases to remain at the forefront of their research.  Bloomberg Government is a premier platform for federal policy, budget, and legislative intelligence. The proposed purchase will enable DHS S&T staff to access real-time government data, track legislative developments, and conduct policy analysis, thereby enhancing operational effectiveness and strategic decision-making. The acquisition is expected to streamline research, improve situational awareness, and support mission-critical activities.  S&T will be procure five  (5) subscriptions for (5) users.

Bloomberg Government (BGOV) is a unique, proprietary platform that provides comprehensive, real-time federal legislative, regulatory, and budgetary intelligence specifically tailored for government affairs professionals. No other commercially available product offers the same breadth and depth of integrated data, advanced analytics, real-time alerts, and customizable dashboards in a single solution. BGOV’s exclusive aggregation of congressional activity, appropriations tracking, policy analysis, and federal contract intelligence is essential for DHS S&T’s mission to monitor and respond to rapidly evolving legislative and regulatory developments. After conducting market research and evaluating alternative so

opps.title: JB,MDL SONET Ring Replacement
opps.description:
The purpose of this Request for Quote is for the acquisition of replace a SONET Ring at Eight (8) each locations, to include SRS to the appropriate GFE CAT 5e, or Thin Net COAX, or Fiber Optic Patch Panels (FOPP) at each location with the applicable Contractor EFI&T patch cords. All FOPP terminations are Snap-and-Twist (ST) connectors. At all locations, cabinet rack space is available for the Contractor to EFI&T mount their equipment. Each cabinet has spare 110VAC, 60Hz recessed female outlets for the Contractor to EFI&T their SRS's power connections or the required Contractor EFI&T UPS. The Contractor shall EFI&T patch cables from these cabinets to the official GFE Demarcation Point, within the serving Comm Room(s). The Government shall provide cut sheets 10 days after contractor award, detailing circuit terminations for each location and the respective patch panels. The Government
requires a commercial, carrier grade SONET/optical transport solution compatible with existing JB MDL network infrastructure throughout Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst (JBMDL), New Jersey, in support of the 87 Communication Squadron (87 CS). This requirement is being solicited in accordance with FAR Part 12, Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services.

See Attch 1 - Statement of Work, for Specific requirements.

opps.description:
NATURE OF REQUIREMENT:

Request for Quote, Security Card Reader System Replacement

In accordance with Federal Supply Schedules Contracts, please submit a quote on the above-mentioned project in accordance with the attached documents. 

DELIVERY AND PERFORMANCE PERIOD REQUIREMENTS

Delivery Date:
The total period of performance for this task order is 30 days after notice to proceed. 

Place of Performance:
All work shall be performed at the following location:
Robert A. Young (RAY) Federal Building 
1222 Spruce Street 
St. Louis, MO 63103

PROPOSAL SUBMISSIONS

Proposals shall consist of the following documents, completed and executed in accordance with this Solicitation:

Price Proposal

The Price Proposal shall consist  a detailed cost breakout. The cost break down shall sum to the total cost of the price proposed for the project. The price proposal acceptance period shall be 90 days from the request for proposal due date.

Deadline for Submissions:

Provide a proposal on or before July 23, at 3:00 p.m. Central Standard Time.  

Submit proposals electronically to the Contracting Officer at: stephan.harris@gsa.gov or uploaded to the GSA eBbuy Website.

Electronic Submission of Proposals

Offers sent shall be deemed timely if received by the email address of the GSA Point of Contact (POC) listed herein on or before the date and time established for receipt of offers, as recorded by the Government computer system's documentation of the exact official time of receipt, not by the time it was sent by the Offeror or received on the GSA server. It is the Offeror's responsibility to ensure proposals are complete and submitted early enough to be received by the GSA POC before the deadline, and to confirm receipt before the deadline. Proposals or portions of proposals which are received by the GSA POC later than the time noted within will not be accepted, reviewed, or considered.

NOTE: The maximum file size is 25 MB, therefore contractors/offerors need to plan accordingly.

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
As stated in the recently completed State Laws Digest for Illegal Passing of School Buses, the term "illegal passing" is a prevalent concept; however, the report illustrated its lack of universality. Variations in state laws dictate that passing a bus from the front on a divided highway may be illegal in some states but permissible under specific roadway conditions in others. What constitutes illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neighboring one. Given the high mobility of the U.S. population, the proximity of urban centers to multiple jurisdictions, and the significant disparities in the content and language of applicable Vehicle and Traffic Laws (VTL), confusion regarding the legality of actions surrounding a school bus is not unexpected.

The lack of clarity in drafting illegal passing provisions further compromises the ability of the typical motorist to understand these VTL requirements. Some jurisdictions have redrafted their amended provisions to include, among other things, the use of cameras. Thus, their updates present a mostly coherent, integrated statement of the law. Other jurisdictions have provisions that appear essentially sequential in design. New or changed provisions simply appear at the end of the existing VTL section, even if they logically relate to a topic much earlier in the material. This type of statutory construction is difficult to follow and can be misleading.

As recommended in the state laws digest, and in anticipation of future legislative interest, this project will focus more deeply on current illegal passing laws and develop a draft model state law to address incidents of illegal passing of stopped school buses. This report will also investigate the potential benefits and feasibility of the citation being deemed a moving violation.

This project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This project will provide if a model law would be n
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is issuing this solicitation to address the growing concern for student safety on and around school buses. The project aims to develop a model law for the illegal passing of school buses, which varies significantly across jurisdictions, leading to confusion and inconsistent enforcement. The initiative is prompted by alarming statistics indicating that illegal passing incidents result in serious injuries and fatalities among children.
